item.title: Rights body witnessed intimidation, whip-wielding vetters at CAB3 hearings
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe’s human rights watchdog has raised the alarm over widespread intimidation and harassment of opponents of Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 during parliamentary public hearings, including the use of men armed with whips to vet participants in Mashonaland West. The Zimbabwe Human Rights Commission (ZHRC) deployed monitoring teams across all provinces from March […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Insisting on a referendum devoid of a legal basis, says AG
item.description: Attorney-General Mrs Virginia Mabiza has dismissed calls for a referendum on the proposed constitutional changes, saying the demands have no legal basis and are driven by political considerations rather than constitutional requirements. In an interview, Mrs Mabiza said the Constitution clearly defines the limited circumstances under which a referendum is required, and argued that the […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Calls Grow for Referendum as Constitutional Amendment Bill Hearings Turn Contentious
item.description: Public hearings on Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 have entered a critical phase, with growing calls from citizens and analysts for the proposed changes to be subjected to a national referendum. The Bill, currently before the Parliament of Zimbabwe, seeks to extend the terms of office for councillors, Members of Parliament and the President […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Chinamasa under fire over ‘colonial sins’ remarks after Coltart assault
item.description: Patrick Chinamasa has ignited controversy after suggesting that human rights lawyer Douglas Coltart was made to “atone” for the alleged colonial legacy of his father following a violent incident in Harare. The remarks come after Coltart was reportedly assaulted by suspected ZANU-PF supporters during a public hearing for Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 at the […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Charamba questions BBC framing of Zimbabwe Constitutional Amendment debate
item.description: Deputy Chief Secretary in the Office of the President and Cabinet (Presidential Communications), George Charamba has criticised a recent BBC report on Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Amendment No. 3 Bill, disputing its characterisation of the reforms as a “power grab.” The BBC story reportedly suggested that the ruling party, ZANU-PF, was seeking to consolidate power through constitutional […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: A new generation is reviving the iPod for distraction-free listening
item.description: Remember the iPod? It’s making a quiet comeback. Four years after Apple killed off its digital music player, secondhand sales are surging. It’s fueled in part by young people interested not just in its retro looks but a desire to listen to music in a focused way and with playlists not determined by algorithms. “There’s […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe to introduce lithium export quotas, sets conditions for resumption of shipments
item.description: Zimbabwe will introduce lithium concentrate export quotas and require commitments for more local processing as part of conditions to allow the resumption of mineral exports, the mines ministry has told producers. Africa’s top lithium producer suspended exports of lithium concentrates and other unprocessed minerals on February 26, after the government alleged malpractices and leakages. In […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Ceasefire in the Iran-Isreal-US war teeters on the brink
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates — A tentative ceasefire in the Iran war staggered Thursday under the weight of Israel’s intense bombardment of Beirut, Tehran’s continued chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz, and uncertainty over whether negotiators can find common ground on a range of other differences. Hours after the ceasefire was announced — amid disagreement […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: CAF president Motsepe calls for unity after AFCON final fracas
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal – Confederation of African Football (CAF) president Patrice Motsepe would welcome an investigation into corruption at the organisation, saying they have nothing to hide following a meeting with Senegalese officials in ​Dakar on Wednesday. Senegal’s government last month demanded an investigation into corruption after the ‌country was stripped of their 2025 Africa Cup […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Chivayo donates Toyota GD6, $50k cash to man whose family was wiped out in crash
item.description: HARARE – Flamboyant businessman Wicknell Chivayo has donated a brand new Toyota Fortuner worth US$80,000 and US$50,000 in cash to a man who lost his wife and all five of his children in a single road traffic accident over the Easter weekend. Ronald Mujuru was left without his entire family after the crash which claimed […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Oil prices rise again and Asian stocks retreat on the fragile Iran ceasefire
item.description: HONG KONG — Oil rose again to above $97 a barrel and Asian stocks were trading mostly lower Thursday on skepticism over a fragile ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran. Investors were closely watching whether a two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran was already slipping after a round of intense Israeli strikes on […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Lawyers win fight over title deeds pilot discrimination
item.description: HARARE – Lawyers on Wednesday warned that the government’s retreat on its handpicked conveyancers for a pilot scheme to digitalise title deeds resolves only part of the problem, and that property owners across Zimbabwe now face a broader fight against a compulsory deeds re-registration programme that could saddle them with costs they never agreed to […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: New Reforms Aim to Revive Zimbabwe's Agriculture Sector
item.description: [263Chat] Government has unveiled sweeping reforms aimed at reducing the cost of doing business in agriculture,a sector it says remains central to the country's economic growth and food security.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe to play Nigeria in 4-nations tournament in London
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe will next month compete in the Unity Cup 2026 in London — the first time Zimbabwe have played in England in a tournament of this nature. The Zimbabwe Football Association confirmed on Wednesday that the Warriors have been included in the four-nation tournament to be held at The Valley, home of Charlton […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Highlanders suspends CEO over Botswana camp ‘financial irregularities’
item.description: BULAWAYO – Highlanders have suspended chief executive officer Denzil Mnkandla over alleged financial mismanagement linked to the club’s recent tour of Botswana, while two other senior officials are facing mounting pressure over the same issue. Mnkandla, who joined the club last year from FC Platinum — becoming Highlanders’ third CEO in 12 months — is […]

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: ZISCO placed under Mutapa Investment Fund in fresh bid to revive collapsed steel giant
item.description: PRESIDENT Emmerson Mnangagwa has moved to place the defunct Zimbabwe Iron and Steel Company (ZISCO) under the Mutapa Investment Fund, in a move aimed at reviving the collapsed steelmaker. The latest amendment means ZISCO, once the backbone of Zimbabwe’s industrial sector but now
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.
channel.item: Rapper Lil Tjay Arrested After Shooting Incident Involving Offset at Florida Casino https://www.mutapatimes.com/articles/2026-04-08-rapper-lil-tjay-arrested-after-shooting-incident-involving-o.html Florida, United States — American rapper Lil Tjay was arrested on Monday night following an altercation outside the Seminole Hard Rock Hotel & Casino, where fellow rapper Offset sustained a gunshot wound. Authorities confirmed that the 24-year-old artist was taken into custody an Thu, 09 Apr 2026 13:16:02 +0000 https://www.mutapatimes.com/articles/2026-04-08-rapper-lil-tjay-arrested-after-shooting-incident-involving-o.html Tech Zimbabwe Mail <media:content url="summary: "Florida, United States — American rapper Lil Tjay was arrested on Monday night following an altercation outside the Seminole Hard Rock Hotel & Casino, where fellow rapper Offset sustained a gunshot wound. Authorities confirmed that the 24-year-old artist was taken into custody an" medium="image"/>

channel.title: The Mutapa Times

item.title: Illegal, Armed, and Dangerous: Chatunga Mugabe Faces These 7 Charges... Here Are His Only 2 Options
item.description: JOHANNESBURG – The youngest son of the late Zimbabwean dictator Robert Mugabe, Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, finds himself at the centre of a serious criminal investigation in South Africa, facing a string of seven charges, including the attempted murder of his gardener, Sipho Mahl
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Air Marshal Henry Muchena is right to stand with Zimbabweans
item.description: At a time when silence would be easier, Air Marshal (Retired) Henry Muchena has chosen principle over comfort and he is right to do so. The post Air Marshal Henry Muchena is right to stand with Zimbabweans appeared first on Nehanda Radio .
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio
